---
name: modern-ui-ux-web
description: Design and implement modern web UI/UX patterns with design systems, accessibility, responsive behavior, and consistent interaction design. Use when creating or reviewing interfaces, page layouts, forms, and component libraries.
---

# Modern UI/UX (Web) Skill

Use this skill when the task is primarily interface quality, usability, or design consistency.

## When to Use This Skill

- Designing or reviewing new pages and complex flows
- Establishing/using a design system with reusable components
- Improving visual hierarchy, spacing, typography, and interaction patterns
- Running an accessibility-first pass on UI work

## Workflow

1. Confirm user goal and primary task per screen
2. Choose or extend existing design system primitives
3. Build mobile-first layout and responsive breakpoints
4. Define clear states: default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error
5. Validate contrast, keyboard access, and readability
6. Review consistency across navigation, forms, feedback, and empty states

## Core Principles

- Clarity over cleverness
- Consistency over novelty
- Accessibility by default
- Fast feedback for every user action
- Progressive disclosure for complexity

## UI Rules

- Use one spacing scale and one typography scale per product area.
- Keep action hierarchy explicit (primary/secondary/tertiary).
- Prefer established component patterns over one-off custom widgets.
- Include skeleton/loading/empty/error states for async screens.
- Preserve predictable navigation and back behavior.

## Accessibility Rules

- Target WCAG AA as baseline.
- Ensure visible focus and keyboard operability.
- Use semantic structure and descriptive labels.
- Avoid color-only status communication.
